item.title: Ukraine Ready to Resume Oil Flows on Druzhba Pipeline, Mol says
item.description: Ukraine is ready to resume oil flows on the Druzhba pipeline, Hungarian refiner Mol Nyrt. said, a step that’s expected to unblock crucial European Union aid to Kyiv on Wednesday.

item.title: AI Firms Seeking Office Space Boost London Developers’ Outlook
item.description: London property developers are benefiting from the rapid expansion of artificial intelligence companies, driving demand for premium office and corporate campus space.

item.title: European Gas Steadies Amid Uncertainty Over Iran Peace Talks
item.description: European natural gas steadied as uncertainty over the Iran war persisted, even as Washington’s truce extension spurred cautious optimism in some markets.

item.title: China’s Oil Majors Sell Barrels as Run Rates Drop to 2022 Low
item.description: Chinese oil majors are selling cargoes of West African and other crudes, according to traders familiar with the deals, a rare move that follows utilization cuts across government-owned refiners as the war in Iran upends global supply.

item.title: Reckitt Sales Hit by Lackluster US Demand for Cold Medicines
item.description: Reckitt Benckiser Group Plc reported weaker-than-expected sales on sluggish demand for its cold medicines in the US and as the conflict in the Middle East hit supplies in the region.

item.title: UK Inflation Hits 3.3% as War Sends Petrol Costs Soaring
item.description: UK inflation accelerated in March, as a surge in energy costs triggered by the Iran war started to hit consumers in the pocket.

item.title: US IPO Candidates Discouraged By Risk of Litigation, Red Tape
item.description: The risk of being sued after an initial public offering is one of the biggest concerns for potential US listing candidates, according to more than 150 executives surveyed by Bloomberg Intelligence.

item.title: Getty Images Downgraded on Weaker Liquidity, Merger Uncertainty
item.description: Moody’s Ratings cut Getty Images Inc.’s credit rating deeper into junk territory, citing weakening liquidity, with lingering uncertainty over a potential merger with Shutterstock Inc. adding to the pressure.

item.title: PBOC Injects Cash Despite Flush Liquidity, Fueling Bond Rally
item.description: China’s central bank has injected more cash into the banking system, signaling unusual tolerance for abundant liquidity and boosting confidence that the bond rally may have further to run.

item.title: UK Inflation Rose to 3.3% in March
Oil Shock Pushes Thailand to Rework Spending to Contain Deficit
item.description: Thailand is reshuffling state spending to cushion the impact of an oil shock while keeping its deficit target intact, highlighting the strain the Middle East conflict is placing on public finances.

item.title: Finland Jobless Rate Rises Again as War in Iran Slows Recovery
item.description: Finland’s unemployment rate rose to the highest level in more than a decade, just as the war in Iran adds to the gloom in the Nordic nation.

item.title: Indonesia Bourse to Remove Tightly Held Firms From Some Indexes
item.description: The Indonesia Stock Exchange plans to remove firms identified for having high shareholding concentration from some of its key indexes as part of ongoing market reforms.

item.title: Danone Sales Rise as Yogurt, Water Outweigh Formula Recalls
item.description: Danone SA sales increased at the start of the year as consumers’ appetite for protein-rich yogurts and Evian bottled water outweighed the impact of an infant formula recall.

item.title: ABB Raises Annual Revenue Outlook as Quarterly Orders Surge
item.description: ABB Ltd. raised its revenue expectations for the year after a jump in orders related to data centers during the first quarter, driving demand for the company’s power-grid products.

item.title: Convenience Store Operator Yesway Raises $280 Million in US IPO
item.description: Convenience store operator Yesway Inc. raised $280 million in an upsized initial public offering priced at the bottom of the marketed range.

item.title: Canada’s TMX Group in Talks to Buy Cboe Australia, AFR Says
item.description: Toronto Stock Exchange owner TMX Group Ltd. has become the preferred bidder to purchase Cboe Global Markets Inc.’s Australian unit, the Australian Financial Review reported, a deal that would offer some certainty for the future of the country’s second-largest exchange operator.

item.title: Nordea First-Quarter Fee Growth Offsets Drop in Lending Income
item.description: Finnish lender Nordea Bank Abp reported profit from lending in the first quarter that declined largely in line with estimates, counterbalanced by profit generated from fees.

item.title: Segantii Wins Permission to Use BofA Documents for Legal Defense
item.description: Segantii Capital Management won a small victory ahead of its insider-trading trial, when a Hong Kong judge said the firm would be permitted to use key documents from Bank of America Corp. as part of its legal defense — something the Wall Street bank had tried to block.

item.title: Iran Tankers Go Dark to Sail Past US Blockade Laden With Crude
item.description: At least two fully laden Iranian tankers have sailed out of the Persian Gulf and past a US blockade this week, part of a flotilla that has made its way around the warships and ferried roughly 9 million barrels of oil to the market.

item.title: Crypto Billionaire Sues Trump-Linked Project Alleging Extortion
item.description: Crypto billionaire Justin Sun and his companies sued World Liberty Financial, a crypto project co-founded by President Donald Trump, accusing the company of extortion and an “illegal scheme” to seize his tokens.

item.title: Korea FX Deposits Slide by Record as Weak Won Spurs Conversions
item.description: South Korea’s foreign-currency deposits fell by a record in March as a weaker won prompted companies to convert holdings into local currency.

item.title: Will Hungary Join the Euro? It’s a Long Road After Orban
item.description: Hungary’s new leader has made adopting the euro one of his top priorities, a step that would mark the largest expansion of the single-currency bloc since Greece joined in 2001.

item.title: The TACO That Ate Market Strategy
item.description: Why ‘buy the dip’ is Trump Always Chickens Out’s best friend.

item.title: Three Bedrooms for €60 Million Shows Monaco’s Dirty Money Headache
item.description: A leaked trove of emails regarding the world’s priciest real estate offers a window into how the principality grapples with money laundering.

item.title: Singapore Says Trade Through Malacca Strait Must Remain Free
item.description: Singapore’s Foreign Minister Vivian Balakrishnan said passage through the straits of Malacca and Singapore, a key trade chokepoint brought into focus by the war in Iran, must remain free for all and that the city-state won’t support any efforts to restrict it.

item.title: China’s Sale of Yuan Bonds in Hong Kong Draws Record-Low Yield
item.description: China’s biggest yuan bond sale in Hong Kong since 2023 drew record-low yields for both two- and 15-year debt, as Beijing speeds up its push to internationalize the currency and mop up pockets of excess liquidity offshore.

item.title: Chinese Regulator Promotes Floating-Rate Bonds to Manage Risks
item.description: China’s interbank bond market regulator is pushing for an increase in the issuance of floating-rate notes to help manage risks from potential interest-rate volatility.

item.title: Cost Blowout Forces Australia to Slash Spending on Disabled
item.description: Australia’s center-left government is moving to rein in the ballooning costs of its signature disability welfare program as it tries to frame next month’s budget against the backdrop of a global energy crisis.

item.title: Traders Push Korea Leveraged Bets to Record as Chip Stocks Rally
item.description: Leveraged bets on South Korean equities jumped to a record as the return of the artificial intelligence trade prompts retail investors to chase a rally in tech heavyweights.
